jokes.text: Wait till he puts his unsuspecting butt on that toilet seat!
jokes.setup: Bart has set up a prank on the toilet seat
jokes.punchline: The anticipation of Homer's reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard prank setup, works for Bart's character but nothing innovative

jokes.text: Huh? Where's the Ee-yow? / I didn't have to. I'm a dude.
jokes.setup: Bart expects Homer to sit and trigger the prank
jokes.punchline: Homer subverts by standing to urinate
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on bathroom habits and foils the expected prank

jokes.text: Butt on bowl, little man! (BOTH STRUGGLING) Hey, my pants aren't down! It doesn't matter!
jokes.setup: Bart forces Homer to sit on the toilet
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of forcing someone fully clothed onto a toilet
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation that's appropriately ridiculous

jokes.text: I'm taking you all out for pancakes. / ALL: Yay! Yeah!
jokes.setup: Tension from the fight and Marge's intervention
jokes.punchline: Homer deflects with random pancake offer that works instantly
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer distraction tactic, family's instant mood shift is believable

jokes.text: It's old lady Simpson! Run!
jokes.setup: Marge approaches during the toilet fight
jokes.punchline: Kids treating Marge like a threatening authority figure
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing kids' perspective on parental authority

jokes.text: They just painted the tower brown. Either way, everyone who works in the kitchen speaks Spanish.
jokes.setup: Restaurant changed from pizza to pancakes
jokes.punchline: Homer's casually racist observation about kitchen staff
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate casual insensitivity but relies on stereotype

jokes.text: The fast track begins at birth. That's it! Claw and bite for position! We have only enough tuition money for two. For the rest of you, better luck next life.
jokes.setup: Competitive pre-nursery school applications
jokes.punchline: Apu's increasingly brutal advice to babies
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that satirizes competitive parenting while staying true to Apu's character

jokes.text: All they should be worried about is the Raspberry Monster! (MARGE BLOWING RASPBERRIES)
jokes.setup: Marge advocates for age-appropriate baby concerns
jokes.punchline: She demonstrates with silly baby game
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that contrasts with competitive parenting satire

jokes.text: Which one? Tri-City, Omni-Pay, lnsta-Threat? I've beaten 'em all!
jokes.setup: Apu threatens collection agency
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud expertise in evading debt collectors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - proud of something he shouldn't be, with specific made-up company names

jokes.text: I could go to any Chuck E. Cheese in America and hear what animals say. Do you know what I say? I say, 'Next.'
jokes.setup: Baby doing basic animal sounds
jokes.punchline: Snobby admission director's dismissive comparison to Chuck E. Cheese
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for the pretentious school director

jokes.text: You're a poopie! / You nurse with that mouth?
jokes.setup: Child calls Henry 'poopie'
jokes.punchline: Henry's witty comeback about nursing
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to 'kiss your mother with that mouth' adapted for babies - clever and economical

jokes.text: Maggie, we're not a mime school, so we can't take a non-talker. But here's some good news. The Gap will always need sweater folders.
jokes.setup: Maggie can't talk yet
jokes.punchline: Condescending career prediction for a baby
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about low expectations for non-verbal babies

jokes.text: She'll have plenty of money, because she'll marry a grocer... who steals from the till. Would ya let me finish? Jeez!
jokes.setup: Homer predicts Maggie will marry a grocer for money
jokes.punchline: The twist that the grocer steals, and Homer's irritation at being interrupted
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Homer's logic and his annoyance at Apu

jokes.text: Rice. Why not caviar?
jokes.setup: Lisa asks for side dish suggestions for doll party
jokes.punchline: Baby Maggie suggests expensive caviar
jokes.explanation: Setup for Maggie's intelligence reveal - baby suggesting sophisticated food

jokes.text: Pippi Non-talking.
jokes.setup: Maggie returns to school
jokes.punchline: Play on 'Pippi Longstocking' referencing her inability to speak
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's economical and shows Henry's pretentious character

jokes.text: Einstein didn't speak till he was three. And even then, he could only speak German.
jokes.setup: Defending Maggie's lack of speech
jokes.punchline: The obvious fact that Einstein spoke German because he was German
jokes.explanation: Marge's innocent logic mistake is endearing and in character

jokes.text: Fine. I'll give her an lQ test if only to extinguish all hope.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to test Maggie
jokes.punchline: Admitting sadistic motivation
jokes.explanation: Shows Henry's cruel nature, honest about his motivations

jokes.text: Well, congratulations. You're now as intelligent as a pig.
jokes.setup: Maggie gets a basic question right
jokes.punchline: Condescending comparison to pig intelligence
jokes.explanation: Reinforces Henry's snobby character with backhanded compliment

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! I got that one right, too. (LAUGHING ENTHUSIASTICALLY)
jokes.setup: Maggie answers family size question
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks he's taking the test too
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his complete misunderstanding of the situation

jokes.text: Your baby is brilliant. Why, she could already teach at Florida State.
jokes.setup: Maggie solves complex arrow puzzle
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Florida State's academic standards
jokes.explanation: Regional college humor that fits Henry's snobbery

jokes.text: One sixty-seven! That's amazing for a Christian.
jokes.setup: Maggie's high IQ is revealed
jokes.punchline: Casual religious prejudice from school director
jokes.explanation: Dark humor establishing the school's elitist prejudices

jokes.text: That's right, because 167 is a bigger number than 159. Do you see how that works?
jokes.setup: Lisa is confused about Maggie being smarter
jokes.punchline: Henry's condescending explanation of basic math
jokes.explanation: Henry's patronizing nature shown through unnecessarily explaining simple math

jokes.text: If we have another one, it could build a time machine, which we could use to go back in time and not have any kids!
jokes.setup: Pattern of increasingly smart kids
jokes.punchline: Homer's paradoxical time travel logic to avoid having kids
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer logic with time travel paradox - original and character-perfect

jokes.text: Welcome to the others. (SCREAMS)
jokes.setup: Marge tries to comfort Lisa about not being the smartest
jokes.punchline: Lisa's horrified realization and scream
jokes.explanation: Economical and dark - Marge's well-meaning but devastating comfort

jokes.text: I'm slapping this sucker right over 'Support Our Troops.'
jokes.setup: Homer has 'Baby Genius' bumper sticker
jokes.punchline: He'll cover his patriotic sticker with it
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's priorities - family bragging over patriotism

jokes.text: Did the last of something die?
jokes.setup: Lisa seems depressed
jokes.punchline: Marge's oddly specific way of asking what's wrong
jokes.explanation: Unusual phrasing that's both specific and absurd - good Marge line

jokes.text: Katie Couric didn't just become the world's most famous dwarf. She escaped from the circus and went for it!
jokes.setup: Giving Lisa encouragement to find new identity
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong 'facts' about Katie Couric
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer misinformation delivered with confidence - absurd but character-perfect

jokes.text: Stuffed cabbage? Can you believe they found a way to make cabbage worse?
jokes.setup: Lisa trying stand-up comedy
jokes.punchline: Joke about stuffed cabbage being worse than regular cabbage
jokes.explanation: Intentionally mediocre joke showing Lisa's failed attempt at stand-up

jokes.text: Hey, why do they call them 'field trips'? We never go to a field. / Oh, untrue. Last Thursday, we visited a battlefield.
jokes.setup: Lisa's observational comedy about field trips
jokes.punchline: Martin's literal correction about battlefield
jokes.explanation: Martin's pedantic correction ruins Lisa's joke - good character interaction

jokes.text: I'm bem-barrassed for you. The following 'ha-ha' is not from amusement but an expression of contempt. Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Martin's response to Lisa's bombing comedy
jokes.punchline: His overly analytical explanation of his laugh
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin - over-explaining humor while missing the point entirely

jokes.text: My new name is Ravencrow Never Smiles.
jokes.setup: Lisa has gone goth
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top goth name
jokes.explanation: Standard goth parody name - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: We'll go to the cemetery and summon the dark lord by kissing and junk.
jokes.setup: Milhouse wants to be goth with Lisa
jokes.punchline: His transparent excuse to kiss Lisa
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse - using any excuse to get close to Lisa

jokes.text: But first, you must apprentice by kissing the goddess lronica, uh, who lives in this rock. Do it for an hour, hour and a half.
jokes.setup: Lisa's response to Milhouse's advance
jokes.punchline: Making him kiss a rock for an extended time
jokes.explanation: Lisa cleverly deflecting Milhouse while maintaining her goth persona

jokes.text: When you're a baby, you spend all day just rolling around on the floor. Boy, I miss that. Ow!
jokes.setup: Homer expressing jealousy of baby life
jokes.punchline: He immediately hurts himself trying it
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish behavior backfiring immediately - good physical comedy setup

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, you're gay. / People who accuse others of being gay are often covering up their own latent homosexuality. / Um, uh... (GRUNTS) Bullies rule!
jokes.setup: Bart makes generic insult
jokes.punchline: Lisa's psychological analysis makes Bart retreat to simpler bullying
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Lisa's intelligence defeating Bart's crude tactics

jokes.text: Huh-Oh-Muh-Eh-Er. That's me. Huh-Oh-Muh-Eh-Er. / This is Huh-Oh-Muh-Eh-Er's doctor. He's too sick to work today.
jokes.setup: Educational toy spells out Homer phonetically
jokes.punchline: Homer pretends to be his own doctor to skip work
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer work avoidance using the phonetic spelling as opportunity

jokes.text: You are purposely teaching her the wrong word!
jokes.setup: Lisa has been sabotaging Maggie's learning
jokes.punchline: Marge catches Lisa in the act
jokes.explanation: Story beat that reveals Lisa's jealousy-driven sabotage

jokes.text: I agree with your mother. You are a disappointment to Huh-Oh-Muh-Eh-Er.
jokes.setup: Phonic Frog commenting on family situation
jokes.punchline: Using the phonetic Homer name in serious context
jokes.explanation: Callback to the phonetic spelling in a serious family moment

jokes.text: Maggie, you're such a quick learner. Why don't you learn how to fly?
jokes.setup: Lisa sarcastically complimenting Maggie
jokes.punchline: Suggesting something impossible/dangerous
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's jealousy turning dark - character development moment

jokes.text: Oh, I thought you said 80. / But she's only eight. / Oh, I thought you said 80. Well, we'll get right on it.
jokes.setup: Wiggum mishears Lisa's age
jokes.punchline: He repeats the same mistake after correction
jokes.explanation: Standard Wiggum incompetence - in character but predictable

jokes.text: 'Somewhat satisfied.' I see. Well, maybe I'll just somewhat find your daughter, huh? / Fine. 'Extremely satisfied.' / Looking good, boys. Now, let's go get some smoothies.
jokes.setup: Survey about police effectiveness during emergency
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's petty response and immediate reward priorities
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment showing his priorities and pettiness

jokes.text: Go ahead and search. You will never find it all. I'm baking muffins as I speak.
jokes.setup: Person caught smuggling food into museum
jokes.punchline: Escalating to cooking inside their clothes
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that's original but doesn't connect to main characters

jokes.text: Some trees are big. Some trees are small. But all trees have bark. Except for poplar, ash and maple.
jokes.setup: Simple tree facts for children
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction of the 'all trees' statement
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction humor that's educationally accurate but basic

jokes.text: Why do you always take the children and not the fondue sets? Why, Santa? Why?
jokes.setup: Reading missing person ads
jokes.punchline: His complaint about missing fondue sets to Santa
jokes.explanation: Good Comic Book Guy moment - self-centered even about tragedies

jokes.text: Well, if you drop a feather and a bowling ball in a vacuum, they'll fall at the same rate. (CHUCKLES) You think you've seen it all in this job, and then something like that comes along.
jokes.setup: Asked what unusual thing they found at museum
jokes.punchline: Wiggum amazed by basic physics instead of addressing the case
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum - distracted by simple science during investigation

jokes.text: Like how does a helicopter fly? And what causes thunder? / I say it's angels bowling.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking basic science questions
jokes.punchline: Lou's folksy explanation for thunder
jokes.explanation: Old-fashioned explanation that fits Lou's character but is predictable

jokes.text: I see a vase, but Eddie sees two people in profile. / Now, this may shock you, but you're both right.
jokes.setup: Police discussing optical illusion during emergency
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's profound delivery of obvious optical illusion explanation
jokes.explanation: Police getting distracted by museum exhibits during rescue

jokes.text: I know, we'll use reverse psychology. Maggie, don't press the red button. / Damn it!
jokes.setup: Homer tries reverse psychology on baby
jokes.punchline: It backfires because Maggie doesn't understand psychology
jokes.explanation: Logic failure that's realistic for the situation

jokes.text: Bart, you were a worthy foe.
jokes.setup: Homer giving death-bed farewells
jokes.punchline: Describing his relationship with his son as adversarial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line - treats parenting like competition even in final moments

jokes.text: Don't try to be something you're not, namely food.
jokes.setup: Homer drawing life lesson from experience
jokes.punchline: The absurd specific lesson about not being food
jokes.explanation: Absurd but logical Homer wisdom - perfect character moment

jokes.text: Your butler let us in. / Play along. I'll explain later.
jokes.setup: Philippa mentions their butler
jokes.punchline: Homer's whispered admission they don't have one
jokes.explanation: Good class confusion comedy with Homer's improvisation

jokes.text: She's as common as an angry woman in an lbsen play. (LAUGHS) Zing!
jokes.setup: Dismissing Maggie's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Pretentious literary reference with self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect pretentious character moment with literary showoff knowledge

jokes.text: She could be at sweeping up hair. (CHUCKLES) / That's my baby, jerk!
jokes.setup: Henry insults Maggie's potential
jokes.punchline: Marge's protective maternal response
jokes.explanation: Good mama bear moment for Marge defending her baby

jokes.text: I felt it, but it was like, 'So what?' / Oh! Again with the nose. I have a chin, you know?
jokes.setup: Henry getting punched by family members
jokes.punchline: His commentary on the fighting technique
jokes.explanation: Henry maintaining his snobby attitude even while being beaten up
